qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stack overflow in recursive normalize() via deeply nested XML elements
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets DOMParser.parseFromString end-of-parse normalization recursion in lib/dom-parser.js and lib/dom.js; the advisory targets unvalidated DocumentType publicId, systemId, and internalSubset serialization in XMLSerializer.serializeToString.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a deep XML nesting denial of service in recursive normalize() during parsing, while GHSA-f6ww-3ggp-fr8h concerns XML injection from verbatim serialization of DocumentType fields by XMLSerializer.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: DOMImplementation.createDocumentType stores publicId, systemId, and internalSubset verbatim, and XMLSerializer's DOCUMENT_TYPE_NODE case emits those fields without validation or escaping.

Finding vulnerable code: DOMParser.parseFromString reaches DOMHandler.endDocument, which calls doc.normalize(); Element.normalize recursively descends deeply nested parsed XML and can throw RangeError.
The finding report does not describe DocumentType field validation, XMLSerializer.serializeToString, DOCTYPE serialization, or early DOCTYPE termination/XML injection.
